expectations.text: Report identifies the Node.js version contradiction (18 vs 20)
expectations.evidence: Contradiction #1 in table: README.md 'Node.js 18 with Express' vs SPEC.md 'Node.js 20 with Fastify'

expectations.text: Report identifies the framework contradiction (Express vs Fastify)
expectations.evidence: Contradiction #1 in table: 'Web framework' — Express vs Fastify

expectations.text: Report identifies the authentication method contradiction (JWT vs session-based)
expectations.evidence: Contradiction #4: 'JWT authentication with 24-hour token expiry, Bearer token' vs 'Session-based authentication with 8-hour session duration, X-API-Key header'

expectations.text: Report identifies the task status values contradiction (at least 2 of the 3 conflicting definitions)
expectations.evidence: Contradiction #3 and #3a identify all three versions: open/in_progress/review/done, todo/doing/review/complete, open/in_progress/done (no review)

expectations.text: Report identifies the rate limit contradiction (100/min vs 60/min)
expectations.evidence: Contradiction #5: '100 requests per minute per user' vs '60 requests per minute per API key'

expectations.text: Report identifies the deployment target contradiction (AWS vs GCP)
expectations.evidence: Contradiction #6: 'AWS ECS, Terraform, infrastructure/' vs 'Google Cloud Run, Pulumi, deploy/'

expectations.text: Each contradiction includes specific file names and quoted values
expectations.evidence: Every table row includes file name, section reference, and exact quoted values

expectations.text: Report assigns severity levels to contradictions
expectations.evidence: Severity column in table: 6 High, 1 Medium, 1 Low

expectations.text: After the report, the skill offers to walk through resolution of each contradiction
expectations.evidence: Full resolution section with 7 numbered contradictions, each presenting Option A/B/C and asking 'Which should we go with?'
